fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Nevada Licensed Dietitian (LD) Application — Requirements & Fees (2026)
seo.description: Apply for a Nevada Licensed Dietitian credential. $200 initial fee + $37.50 background check. Master's degree, CDR exam, and 1,200 hours of supervised practice required.
seo.keywords: Nevada dietitian license, NV licensed dietitian requirements, Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health Dietitians, Nevada dietitian application fee

board.name: Nevada Division of Public and Behavioral Health — Dietitian Licensing
board.parent_dept: Department of Health and Human Services

quick_facts.fee_breakdown: $200 initial application fee + $37.50 fingerprint background check fee
quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (no state-mandated CE, but CDR requires 75 CPEUs per 5 years)

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Nevada, regulated by the Division of Public and Behavioral Health.

license_types.title: Provisional Dietitian License
license_types.description: Available for applicants who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $200, and allows practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

license_types.title: Temporary Dietitian License
license_types.description: Available for out-of-state dietitians practicing in Nevada for a short term. Valid for up to 6 months and costs $25.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 1,200 hours of supervised practice.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians.

steps.title: Submit the Application via Nevada eLicensing
steps.description: Create an account on the Nevada online portal, complete the application, and pay the $200 initial application fee.

steps.title: Complete Background Check & Provide Documents
steps.description: Submit fingerprints to the Central Repository for Nevada Records of Criminal History ($37.50 fee). Upload a copy of your current CDR registration card and official transcripts.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.
Verification sent directly from the Nevada Department of Public Safety.
Valid government-issued photo identification and evidence of lawful presence.

provisional_text: Nevada offers a provisional license ($200 fee) for individuals who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but are waiting to take the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an active Nevada Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year.
reciprocity_text: Applicants actively licensed in another state can easily apply for Nevada endorsement by providing verification of their current active license, submitting the standard application, paying the $200 fee, and completing the fingerprint background check.

faqs.question: How much does a Nevada dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is $200, plus a $37.50 fee for the fingerprint background check. The provisional license also costs $200.

faqs.question: When does the Nevada license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Nevada dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years). The renewal fee is $200.

faqs.question: What are the continuing education requirements for Nevada?
faqs.answer: Nevada currently relies on the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) for continuing education tracking. There are no additional state-mandated CE hours beyond the 75 CPEUs required every five years to maintain active CDR registration.
